tEn: Switch Transformers: Scaling to Trillion Parameter Models with Simple and Efficient Sparsity
tZh: Switch Transformers：通过简单高效的稀疏性扩展到万亿参数模型
sEn: Switch Transformers simplify MoE routing, reduce communication and training instability, enabling trillion-parameter models with up to 7x speedup.
sZh: Switch Transformers简化了混合专家路由，降低了通信成本和训练不稳定性，实现了万亿参数模型，速度提升高达7倍。
absEn: In deep learning, models typically reuse the same parameters for all inputs. Mixture of Experts (MoE) defies this and instead selects different parameters for each incoming example. The result is a sparsely-activated model -- with outrageous numbers of parameters -- but a constant computational cost. However, despite several notable successes of MoE, widespread adoption has been hindered by complexity, communication costs and training instability -- we address these with the Switch Transformer. We simplify the MoE routing algorithm and design intuitive improved models with reduced communication and computational costs. Our proposed training techniques help wrangle the instabilities and we show large sparse models may be trained, for the first time, with lower precision (bfloat16) formats. We design models based off T5-Base and T5-Large to obtain up to 7x increases in pre-training speed with the same computational resources. These improvements extend into multilingual settings where we measure gains over the mT5-Base version across all 101 languages. Finally, we advance the current scale of language models by pre-training up to trillion parameter models on the "Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus" and achieve a 4x speedup over the T5-XXL model.
absZh: 在深度学习中，模型通常对所有输入重用相同的参数。混合专家（MoE）打破了这一惯例，为每个输入示例选择不同的参数。结果是一个稀疏激活的模型——参数数量惊人——但计算成本恒定。然而，尽管MoE取得了一些显著的成功，但其复杂性、通信成本和训练不稳定性阻碍了广泛采用——我们通过Switch Transformer解决了这些问题。我们简化了MoE路由算法，并设计了直观的改进模型，减少了通信和计算成本。我们提出的训练技术有助于控制不稳定性，并首次展示了可以使用较低精度（bfloat16）格式训练大型稀疏模型。我们基于T5-Base和T5-Large设计的模型在相同计算资源下实现了高达7倍的预训练速度提升。这些改进扩展到多语言设置，我们在所有101种语言上测量了相对于mT5-Base版本的增益。最后，我们通过在“Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus”上预训练高达万亿参数的模型，推进了当前语言模型的规模，并实现了比T5-XXL模型快4倍的速度。

contrib.en: Simplifies MoE routing to top-1 expert, reducing computation and communication costs.
contrib.zh: 将 MoE 路由简化为 top-1 专家，降低计算和通信成本。

contrib.en: Achieves up to 7x pre-training speedup over T5 with same FLOPs per token.
contrib.zh: 在相同每 token FLOPs 下，预训练速度相比 T5 提升高达 7 倍。

contrib.en: Introduces selective precision (bfloat16) training for large sparse models.
contrib.zh: 引入选择性精度（bfloat16）训练大型稀疏模型。

contrib.en: Demonstrates scaling to trillion parameters with 4x speedup over T5-XXL.
contrib.zh: 展示扩展到万亿参数，相比 T5-XXL 实现 4 倍加速。

contrib.en: Shows universal improvements across 101 languages in multilingual setting.
contrib.zh: 在多语言设置中，所有 101 种语言均获得改进。

contrib.en: Proposes expert dropout and distillation to preserve 30% quality gain with 99% compression.
contrib.zh: 提出专家 dropout 和蒸馏，以 99% 压缩率保留 30% 的质量提升。

limits.en: Training instability persists for largest models like Switch-XXL.
limits.zh: 最大模型（如 Switch-XXL）仍存在训练不稳定性。

limits.en: Downstream gains do not fully match upstream perplexity improvements.
limits.zh: 下游任务收益未能完全匹配上游困惑度改进。

limits.en: Fine-tuning quality depends on FLOPs per token and parameter count in unclear ways.
limits.zh: 微调质量与每 token FLOPs 和参数数量的关系尚不明确。

limits.en: Expert capacity factor introduces trade-off between token dropping and efficiency.
limits.zh: 专家容量因子在 token 丢弃和效率之间存在权衡。

limits.en: Distillation preserves only ~30% of sparse model quality gain.
limits.zh: 蒸馏仅保留约 30% 的稀疏模型质量提升。

paras.en: In deep learning, models typically reuse the same parameters for all inputs. Mixture of Experts (MoE) models defy this and instead select different parameters for each incoming example. The result is a sparsely-activated model—with an outrageous number of parameters—but a constant computational cost. However, despite several notable successes of MoE, widespread adoption has been hindered by complexity, communication costs, and training instability. We address these with the introduction of the Switch Transformer. We simplify the MoE routing algorithm and design intuitive improved models with reduced communication and computational costs. Our proposed training techniques mitigate the instabilities, and we show large sparse models may be trained, for the first time, with lower precision (bfloat16) formats. We design models based off T5-Base and T5-Large (Raffel et al., 2019) to obtain up to 7x increases in pre-training speed with the same computational resources. These improvements extend into multilingual settings where we measure gains over the mT5-Base version across all 101 languages. Finally, we advance the current scale of language models by pre-training up to trillion parameter models on the “Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus”, and achieve a 4x speedup over the T5-XXL model.111JAX code for Switch Transformer and all model checkpoints are available at https://github.com/google-research/t5x222Tensorflow code for Switch Transformer is available at https://github.com/tensorflow/mesh/blob/master/mesh_tensorflow/transformer/moe.py
paras.zh: 在深度学习中，模型通常对所有输入重复使用相同的参数。混合专家（MoE）模型则打破这一常规，为每个输入样本选择不同的参数。结果得到一个稀疏激活的模型——参数数量惊人——但计算成本恒定。然而，尽管MoE取得了几项显著成功，其广泛采用仍受到复杂性、通信成本和训练不稳定的阻碍。我们通过引入Switch Transformer来解决这些问题。我们简化了MoE路由算法，并设计了直观改进的模型，降低了通信和计算成本。我们提出的训练技术缓解了不稳定性，并首次展示了大规模稀疏模型可以使用低精度（bfloat16）格式进行训练。我们基于T5-Base和T5-Large（Raffel等人，2019）设计模型，在相同计算资源下实现了高达7倍的预训练速度提升。这些改进扩展到多语言设置，我们在所有101种语言上测量到相对于mT5-Base版本的提升。最后，我们通过在“Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus”上预训练高达万亿参数的模型，推进了当前语言模型的规模，并实现了比T5-XXL模型4倍的加速。

paras.en: Keywords: mixture-of-experts, natural language processing, sparsity, large-scale machine learning, distributed computing
paras.zh: 关键词：混合专家、自然语言处理、稀疏性、大规模机器学习、分布式计算

paras.en: Large scale training has been an effective path towards flexible and powerful neural language models (Radford et al., 2018; Kaplan et al., 2020; Brown et al., 2020). Simple architectures—backed by a generous computational budget, data set size and parameter count—surpass more complicated algorithms (Sutton, 2019). An approach followed in Radford et al. (2018); Raffel et al. (2019); Brown et al. (2020) expands the model size of a densely-activated Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017). While effective, it is also extremely computationally intensive (Strubell et al., 2019). Inspired by the success of model scale, but seeking greater computational efficiency, we instead propose a sparsely-activated expert model: the Switch Transformer. In our case the sparsity comes from activating a subset of the neural network weights for each incoming example.
paras.zh: 大规模训练一直是实现灵活且强大的神经语言模型的有效途径（Radford 等，2018；Kaplan 等，2020；Brown 等，2020）。在充足的计算预算、数据集规模和参数数量的支持下，简单的架构超越了更复杂的算法（Sutton，2019）。Radford 等（2018）、Raffel 等（2019）和 Brown 等（2020）采用的方法扩展了密集激活 Transformer（Vaswani 等，2017）的模型规模。虽然有效，但这种方法也极其计算密集（Strubell 等，2019）。受模型规模成功经验的启发，但为了追求更高的计算效率，我们转而提出一种稀疏激活的专家模型：Switch Transformer。在我们的案例中，稀疏性来自于为每个输入示例激活神经网络权重的一个子集。

paras.en: Sparse training is an active area of research and engineering (Gray et al., 2017; Gale et al., 2020), but as of today, machine learning libraries and hardware accelerators still cater to dense matrix multiplications. To have an efficient sparse algorithm, we start with the Mixture-of-Expert (MoE) paradigm (Jacobs et al., 1991; Jordan and Jacobs, 1994; Shazeer et al., 2017), and simplify it to yield training stability and computational benefits. MoE models have had notable successes in machine translation (Shazeer et al., 2017, 2018; Lepikhin et al., 2020), however, widespread adoption is hindered by complexity, communication costs, and training instabilities.
paras.zh: 稀疏训练是一个活跃的研究和工程领域（Gray 等，2017；Gale 等，2020），但时至今日，机器学习库和硬件加速器仍然服务于密集矩阵乘法。为了获得高效的稀疏算法，我们从混合专家（MoE）范式（Jacobs 等，1991；Jordan 和 Jacobs，1994；Shazeer 等，2017）出发，并对其进行简化以实现训练稳定性和计算效益。MoE 模型在机器翻译方面取得了显著成功（Shazeer 等，2017，2018；Lepikhin 等，2020），然而，由于复杂性、通信成本和训练不稳定性，其广泛采用受到阻碍。

paras.en: We address these issues, and then go beyond translation, to find that these class of algorithms are broadly valuable in natural language. We measure superior scaling on a diverse set of natural language tasks and across three regimes in NLP: pre-training, fine-tuning and multi-task training. While this work focuses on scale, we also show that the Switch Transformer architecture not only excels in the domain of supercomputers, but is beneficial even with only a few computational cores. Further, our large sparse models can be distilled (Hinton et al., 2015) into small dense versions while preserving 30% of the sparse model quality gain. Our contributions are the following:
paras.zh: 我们解决了这些问题，然后超越翻译领域，发现这类算法在自然语言处理中具有广泛价值。我们在多种自然语言任务上以及 NLP 的三个阶段（预训练、微调和多任务训练）中测量了优越的缩放特性。虽然本文主要关注规模，但我们也表明 Switch Transformer 架构不仅在超级计算机领域表现出色，即使在只有少量计算核心的情况下也很有益。此外，我们的大型稀疏模型可以蒸馏（Hinton 等，2015）成小型密集版本，同时保留 30% 的稀疏模型质量提升。我们的贡献如下：

paras.en: The Switch Transformer architecture, which simplifies and improves over Mixture of Experts.
paras.zh: Switch Transformer 架构，它简化并改进了混合专家模型。

paras.en: Scaling properties and a benchmark against the strongly tuned T5 model (Raffel et al., 2019) where we measure 7x+ pre-training speedups while still using the same FLOPS per token. We further show the improvements hold even with limited computational resources, using as few as two experts.
paras.zh: 缩放特性以及与经过强调优的 T5 模型（Raffel 等，2019）的基准比较，我们测量到预训练速度提升 7 倍以上，同时每个 token 的 FLOPS 保持不变。我们进一步表明，即使在计算资源有限的情况下，仅使用两个专家，这种改进仍然成立。

paras.en: Successful distillation of sparse pre-trained and specialized fine-tuned models into small dense models. We reduce the model size by up to 99% while preserving 30% of the quality gains of the large sparse teacher.
paras.zh: 成功地将稀疏预训练和专门微调的模型蒸馏成小型密集模型。我们将模型大小减少高达 99%，同时保留了大型稀疏教师模型 30% 的质量提升。

paras.en: Improved pre-training and fine-tuning techniques: (1) selective precision training that enables training with lower bfloat16 precision (2) an initialization scheme that allows for scaling to a larger number of experts and (3) increased expert regularization that improves sparse model fine-tuning and multi-task training.
paras.zh: 改进的预训练和微调技术：（1）选择性精度训练，使得能够使用较低的 bfloat16 精度进行训练；（2）一种初始化方案，允许扩展到更多专家；（3）增加专家正则化，改善稀疏模型的微调和多任务训练。

paras.en: A measurement of the pre-training benefits on multilingual data where we find a universal improvement across all 101 languages and with 91% of languages benefiting from 4x+ speedups over the mT5 baseline (Xue et al., 2020).
paras.zh: 在多语言数据上测量预训练收益，我们发现所有 101 种语言都有普遍改进，其中 91% 的语言相比 mT5 基线（Xue 等，2020）获得了 4 倍以上的加速。

paras.en: An increase in the scale of neural language models achieved by efficiently combining data, model, and expert-parallelism to create models with up to a trillion parameters. These models improve the pre-training speed of a strongly tuned T5-XXL baseline by 4x.
paras.zh: 通过有效结合数据、模型和专家并行性，实现了神经语言模型规模的增加，创建了参数多达一万亿的模型。这些模型将经过强调优的 T5-XXL 基线的预训练速度提高了 4 倍。

paras.en: The guiding design principle for Switch Transformers is to maximize the parameter count of a Transformer model (Vaswani et al., 2017) in a simple and computationally efficient way. The benefit of scale was exhaustively studied in Kaplan et al. (2020) which uncovered power-law scaling with model size, data set size and computational budget. Importantly, this work advocates training large models on relatively small amounts of data as the computationally optimal approach.
paras.zh: Switch Transformer 的指导设计原则是以简单且计算高效的方式最大化 Transformer 模型（Vaswani 等人，2017）的参数数量。规模的好处已在 Kaplan 等人（2020）的研究中得到详尽探讨，该研究揭示了模型大小、数据集大小和计算预算之间的幂律缩放关系。重要的是，该工作主张在相对较少的数据上训练大型模型是计算上最优的方法。

paras.en: Heeding these results, we investigate a fourth axis: increase the parameter count while keeping the floating point operations (FLOPs) per example constant. Our hypothesis is that the parameter count, independent of total computation performed, is a separately important axis on which to scale. We achieve this by designing a sparsely activated model that efficiently uses hardware designed for dense matrix multiplications such as GPUs and TPUs. Our work here focuses on TPU architectures, but these class of models may be similarly trained on GPU clusters. In our distributed training setup, our sparsely activated layers split unique weights on different devices. Therefore, the weights of the model increase with the number of devices, all while maintaining a manageable memory and computational footprint on each device.
paras.zh: 遵循这些结果，我们研究了第四个维度：在保持每个示例的浮点运算次数（FLOPs）不变的情况下增加参数数量。我们的假设是，参数数量（独立于执行的总计算量）是一个单独重要的缩放轴。我们通过设计一种稀疏激活模型来实现这一点，该模型高效地利用了为密集矩阵乘法设计的硬件，如 GPU 和 TPU。我们的工作主要关注 TPU 架构，但这类模型也可以类似地在 GPU 集群上训练。在我们的分布式训练设置中，我们的稀疏激活层将不同的权重分配到不同的设备上。因此，模型的权重随着设备数量的增加而增加，同时每个设备上的内存和计算开销保持在可控范围内。

paras.en: Switch Routing: Rethinking Mixture-of-Experts. Shazeer et al. (2017) conjectured that routing to k>1𝑘1k>1 experts was necessary in order to have non-trivial gradients to the routing functions. The authors intuited that learning to route would not work without the ability to compare at least two experts. Ramachandran and Le (2018) went further to study the top-k𝑘k decision and found that higher k𝑘k-values in lower layers in the model were important for models with many routing layers. Contrary to these ideas, we instead use a simplified strategy where we route to only a single expert. We show this simplification preserves model quality, reduces routing computation and performs better. This k=1𝑘1k=1 routing strategy is later referred to as a Switch layer. Note that for both MoE and Switch Routing, the gate value pi​(x)subscript𝑝𝑖𝑥p_{i}(x) in Equation 2 permits differentiability of the router.
paras.zh: Switch路由：重新思考混合专家。Shazeer等人（2017）推测，路由到k>1个专家对于路由函数获得非平凡梯度是必要的。作者直觉认为，如果没有至少两个专家的比较，学习路由将无法工作。Ramachandran和Le（2018）进一步研究了top-k决策，发现在具有多个路由层的模型中，较低层中较高的k值很重要。与这些想法相反，我们采用了一种简化策略，即仅路由到单个专家。我们证明这种简化保持了模型质量，减少了路由计算，并且性能更好。这种k=1的路由策略后来被称为Switch层。注意，对于MoE和Switch路由，公式2中的门控值p_i(x)都允许路由器的可微性。

paras.en: The benefits for the Switch layer are three-fold: (1) The router computation is reduced as we are only routing a token to a single expert. (2) The batch size (expert capacity) of each expert can be at least halved since each token is only being routed to a single expert.333See Section 2.2 for a technical description. (3) The routing implementation is simplified and communication costs are reduced. Figure 3 shows an example of routing with different expert capacity factors.
paras.zh: Switch层的优势有三点：（1）路由器计算减少，因为我们只将词元路由到单个专家。（2）每个专家的批量大小（专家容量）至少可以减半，因为每个词元只被路由到一个专家。³（3）路由实现简化，通信成本降低。图3展示了不同专家容量因子下的路由示例。

paras.en: We use Mesh-Tensorflow (MTF) (Shazeer et al., 2018) which is a library, with similar semantics and API to Tensorflow (Abadi et al., 2016) that facilitates efficient distributed data and model parallel architectures. It does so by abstracting the physical set of cores to a logical mesh of processors. Tensors and computations may then be sharded per named dimensions, facilitating easy partitioning of models across dimensions. We design our model with TPUs in mind, which require statically declared sizes. Below we describe our distributed Switch Transformer implementation.
paras.zh: 我们使用 Mesh-Tensorflow (MTF) (Shazeer et al., 2018)，这是一个与 Tensorflow (Abadi et al., 2016) 具有相似语义和 API 的库，有助于实现高效的分布式数据和模型并行架构。它通过将物理核心集抽象为逻辑处理器网格来实现这一点。张量和计算随后可以按命名维度进行分片，从而便于跨维度轻松划分模型。我们在设计模型时考虑了 TPU，这需要静态声明大小。下面我们描述分布式 Switch Transformer 的实现。

paras.en: Distributed Switch Implementation. All of our tensor shapes are statically determined at compilation time, but our computation is dynamic due to the routing decisions at training and inference. Because of this, one important technical consideration is how to set the expert capacity. The expert capacity—the number of tokens each expert computes—is set by evenly dividing the number of tokens in the batch across the number of experts, and then further expanding by a capacity factor,
paras.zh: 分布式 Switch 实现。我们所有的张量形状在编译时都是静态确定的，但由于训练和推理时的路由决策，我们的计算是动态的。因此，一个重要的技术考虑是如何设置专家容量。专家容量——每个专家计算的 token 数量——通过将批次中的 token 数量均匀分配到专家数量上，然后进一步乘以容量因子来设置。

paras.en: A capacity factor greater than 1.0 creates additional buffer to accommodate for when tokens are not perfectly balanced across experts. If too many tokens are routed to an expert (referred to later as dropped tokens), computation is skipped and the token representation is passed directly to the next layer through the residual connection. Increasing the expert capacity is not without drawbacks, however, since high values will result in wasted computation and memory. This trade-off is explained in Figure 3. Empirically we find ensuring lower rates of dropped tokens are important for the scaling of sparse expert-models. Throughout our experiments we didn’t notice any dependency on the number of experts for the number of tokens dropped (typically <1%absentpercent1<1\%). Using the auxiliary load balancing loss (next section) with a high enough coefficient ensured good load balancing. We study the impact that these design decisions have on model quality and speed in Table 1.
paras.zh: 大于 1.0 的容量因子会创建额外的缓冲区，以应对 token 在专家之间不完全平衡的情况。如果太多 token 被路由到某个专家（后面称为丢弃的 token），则跳过计算，并通过残差连接将 token 表示直接传递到下一层。然而，增加专家容量并非没有缺点，因为高值会导致计算和内存的浪费。这种权衡在图 3 中解释。根据经验，我们发现确保较低的 token 丢弃率对于稀疏专家模型的缩放很重要。在我们的实验中，我们没有注意到丢弃的 token 数量与专家数量有任何依赖关系（通常 <1%）。使用足够高系数的辅助负载平衡损失（下一节）确保了良好的负载平衡。我们在表 1 中研究了这些设计决策对模型质量和速度的影响。

paras.en: A Differentiable Load Balancing Loss. To encourage a balanced load across experts we add an auxiliary loss (Shazeer et al., 2017, 2018; Lepikhin et al., 2020). As in Shazeer et al. (2018); Lepikhin et al. (2020), Switch Transformers simplifies the original design in Shazeer et al. (2017) which had separate load-balancing and importance-weighting losses. For each Switch layer, this auxiliary loss is added to the total model loss during training. Given N𝑁N experts indexed by i=1𝑖1i=1 to N𝑁N and a batch ℬℬ\mathcal{B} with T𝑇T tokens, the auxiliary loss is computed as the scaled dot-product between vectors f𝑓f and P𝑃P,
paras.zh: 可微分的负载平衡损失。为了鼓励专家之间的负载平衡，我们添加了一个辅助损失 (Shazeer et al., 2017, 2018; Lepikhin et al., 2020)。与 Shazeer et al. (2018); Lepikhin et al. (2020) 一样，Switch Transformers 简化了 Shazeer et al. (2017) 中原始的设计，后者具有单独的负载平衡和重要性加权损失。对于每个 Switch 层，该辅助损失在训练期间被添加到总模型损失中。给定 N 个专家，索引 i=1 到 N，以及一个包含 T 个 token 的批次 B，辅助损失计算为向量 f 和 P 之间的缩放点积，

paras.en: where fisubscript𝑓𝑖f_{i} is the fraction of tokens dispatched to expert i𝑖i,
paras.zh: 其中 f_i 是分配给专家 i 的 token 比例，

paras.en: and Pisubscript𝑃𝑖P_{i} is the fraction of the router probability allocated for expert i𝑖i, 222A potential source of confusion: pi​(x)subscript𝑝𝑖𝑥p_{i}(x) is the probability of routing token x𝑥x to expert i𝑖i. Pisubscript𝑃𝑖P_{i} is the probability fraction to expert i𝑖i across all tokens in the batch ℬℬ\mathcal{B}.
paras.zh: 而 P_i 是分配给专家 i 的路由器概率比例，

paras.en: Since we seek uniform routing of the batch of tokens across the N𝑁N experts, we desire both vectors to have values of 1/N1𝑁1/N. The auxiliary loss of Equation 4 encourages uniform routing since it is minimized under a uniform distribution. The objective can also be differentiated as the P𝑃P-vector is differentiable, but the f𝑓f-vector is not. The final loss is multiplied by expert count N𝑁N to keep the loss constant as the number of experts varies since under uniform routing ∑i=1N(fi⋅Pi)=∑i=1N(1N⋅1N)=1Nsuperscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑁⋅subscript𝑓𝑖subscript𝑃𝑖superscriptsubscript𝑖1𝑁⋅1𝑁1𝑁1𝑁\sum_{i=1}^{N}(f_{i}\cdot P_{i})=\sum_{i=1}^{N}(\frac{1}{N}\cdot\frac{1}{N})=\frac{1}{N}. Finally, a hyper-parameter α𝛼\alpha is a multiplicative coefficient for these auxiliary losses; throughout this work we use an α=10−2𝛼superscript102\alpha=10^{-2} which was sufficiently large to ensure load balancing while small enough to not to overwhelm the primary cross-entropy objective. We swept hyper-parameter ranges of α𝛼\alpha from 10−1superscript10110^{-1} to 10−5superscript10510^{-5} in powers of 10 and found 10−2superscript10210^{-2} balanced load quickly without interfering with training loss.
paras.zh: 由于我们寻求将批次 token 均匀路由到 N 个专家，我们希望两个向量的值都为 1/N。公式 4 的辅助损失鼓励均匀路由，因为它在均匀分布下最小化。该目标也可以微分，因为 P 向量是可微的，但 f 向量不是。最终损失乘以专家数量 N，以保持损失在专家数量变化时恒定，因为在均匀路由下，∑_{i=1}^N (f_i · P_i) = ∑_{i=1}^N (1/N · 1/N) = 1/N。最后，超参数 α 是这些辅助损失的乘法系数；在整个工作中，我们使用 α=10^{-2}，它足够大以确保负载平衡，同时足够小以避免压倒主要的交叉熵目标。我们以 10 的幂次从 10^{-1} 到 10^{-5} 扫描了 α 的超参数范围，发现 10^{-2} 能快速平衡负载而不干扰训练损失。

full.sec: 2.3 Putting It All Together: The Switch Transformer

paras.en: Our first test of the Switch Transformer starts with pre-training on the “Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus” (C4), introduced in (Raffel et al., 2019). For our pre-training objective, we use a masked language modeling task (Taylor, 1953; Fedus et al., 2018; Devlin et al., 2018) where the model is trained to predict missing tokens. In our pre-training setting, as determined in Raffel et al. (2019) to be optimal, we drop out 15% of tokens and then replace the masked sequence with a single sentinel token. To compare our models, we record the negative log perplexity.444We use log base-e𝑒e for this metric so the units are nats. Throughout all tables in the paper, ↑↑\uparrow indicates that a higher value for that metric is better and vice-versa for ↓↓\downarrow. A comparison of all the models studied in this work are in Table 9.
paras.zh: 我们对 Switch Transformer 的首次测试始于在 (Raffel et al., 2019) 中引入的“Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus”（C4）上的预训练。对于预训练目标，我们使用掩码语言建模任务 (Taylor, 1953; Fedus et al., 2018; Devlin et al., 2018)，其中模型被训练来预测缺失的 token。在我们的预训练设置中，根据 Raffel et al. (2019) 确定的最优方案，我们丢弃了 15% 的 token，然后将掩码序列替换为单个哨兵 token。为了比较我们的模型，我们记录了负对数困惑度。444我们使用以 e 为底的对数，因此单位是 nats。在本文的所有表格中，↑ 表示该指标值越高越好，反之 ↓ 表示越低越好。本文研究的所有模型的比较见表 9。

paras.en: A head-to-head comparison of the Switch Transformer and the MoE Transformer is presented in Table 1. Our Switch Transformer model is FLOP-matched to ‘T5-Base’ (Raffel et al., 2019) (same amount of computation per token is applied). The MoE Transformer, using top-2 routing, has two experts which each apply a separate FFN to each token and thus its FLOPS are larger. All models were trained for the same number of steps on identical hardware. Note that the MoE model going from capacity factor 2.0 to 1.25 actually slows down (840 to 790) in the above experiment setup, which is unexpected.555Note that speed measurements are both a function of the algorithm and the implementation details. Switch Transformer reduces the necessary computation relative to MoE (algorithm), but the final speed differences are impacted by low-level optimizations (implementation).
paras.zh: Switch Transformer 与 MoE Transformer 的直接比较见表 1。我们的 Switch Transformer 模型在 FLOP 上与 'T5-Base' (Raffel et al., 2019) 匹配（每个 token 应用的计算量相同）。使用 top-2 路由的 MoE Transformer 有两个专家，每个专家对每个 token 应用单独的 FFN，因此其 FLOP 更大。所有模型在相同硬件上训练相同的步数。注意，在上述实验设置中，MoE 模型从容量因子 2.0 变为 1.25 时实际上变慢了（从 840 到 790），这出乎意料。555注意，速度测量既是算法也是实现细节的函数。Switch Transformer 相对于 MoE 减少了必要的计算（算法），但最终的速度差异受到底层优化（实现）的影响。

paras.en: We highlight three key findings from Table 1: (1) Switch Transformers outperform both carefully tuned dense models and MoE Transformers on a speed-quality basis. For a fixed amount of computation and wall-clock time, Switch Transformers achieve the best result. (2) The Switch Transformer has a smaller computational footprint than the MoE counterpart. If we increase its size to match the training speed of the MoE Transformer, we find this outperforms all MoE and Dense models on a per step basis as well. (3) Switch Transformers perform better at lower capacity factors (1.0, 1.25). Smaller expert capacities are indicative of the scenario in the large model regime where model memory is very scarce and the capacity factor will want to be made as small as possible.
paras.zh: 我们从表 1 中强调三个关键发现：（1）在速度-质量基础上，Switch Transformer 优于精心调整的密集模型和 MoE Transformer。在固定的计算量和挂钟时间内，Switch Transformer 取得了最佳结果。（2）Switch Transformer 的计算足迹比 MoE 对应模型更小。如果我们增加其大小以匹配 MoE Transformer 的训练速度，我们发现它在每步基础上也优于所有 MoE 和密集模型。（3）Switch Transformer 在较低的容量因子（1.0, 1.25）下表现更好。较小的专家容量表明在大型模型场景中，模型内存非常稀缺，容量因子希望尽可能小。

full.secZh: 2.4 改进的训练与微调技术

paras.en: Sparse expert models may introduce training difficulties over a vanilla Transformer. Instability can result because of the hard-switching (routing) decisions at each of these layers. Further, low precision formats like bfloat16 (Wang and Kanwar, 2019) can exacerbate issues in the softmax computation for our router. We describe training difficulties here and the methods we use to overcome them to achieve stable and scalable training.
paras.zh: 稀疏专家模型可能比普通 Transformer 引入训练困难。由于每一层的硬切换（路由）决策，可能导致不稳定性。此外，像 bfloat16（Wang 和 Kanwar, 2019）这样的低精度格式可能加剧路由器 softmax 计算中的问题。我们在此描述训练困难以及我们用于克服这些困难以实现稳定和可扩展训练的方法。

paras.en: Selective precision with large sparse models. Model instability hinders the ability to train using efficient bfloat16 precision, and as a result, Lepikhin et al. (2020) trains with float32 precision throughout their MoE Transformer. However, we show that by instead selectively casting to float32 precision within a localized part of the model, stability may be achieved, without incurring expensive communication cost of float32 tensors. This technique is inline with modern mixed precision training strategies where certain parts of the model and gradient updates are done in higher precision Micikevicius et al. (2017). Table 2 shows that our approach permits nearly equal speed to bfloat16 training while conferring the training stability of float32.
paras.zh: 大型稀疏模型的选择性精度。模型不稳定性阻碍了使用高效 bfloat16 精度进行训练的能力，因此 Lepikhin 等人（2020）在整个 MoE Transformer 中使用 float32 精度进行训练。然而，我们表明，通过在模型的局部区域选择性地转换为 float32 精度，可以实现稳定性，而无需承担 float32 张量的昂贵通信成本。该技术符合现代混合精度训练策略，其中模型的某些部分和梯度更新以更高精度进行（Micikevicius 等人，2017）。表 2 显示，我们的方法在实现 float32 的训练稳定性的同时，速度几乎与 bfloat16 训练相当。

paras.en: To achieve this, we cast the router input to float32 precision. The router function takes the tokens as input and produces the dispatch and combine tensors used for the selection and recombination of expert computation (refer to Code Block 15 in the Appendix for details). Importantly, the float32 precision is only used within the body of the router function—on computations local to that device. Because the resulting dispatch and combine tensors are recast to bfloat16 precision at the end of the function, no expensive float32 tensors are broadcast through all-to-all communication operations, but we still benefit from the increased stability of float32.
paras.zh: 为了实现这一点，我们将路由器输入转换为 float32 精度。路由器函数将 token 作为输入，并生成用于选择和重组专家计算的调度和组合张量（详情请参见附录中的代码块 15）。重要的是，float32 精度仅在路由器函数体内使用——在设备本地的计算上。由于生成的调度和组合张量在函数末尾被重新转换为 bfloat16 精度，因此没有昂贵的 float32 张量通过全对全通信操作广播，但我们仍然受益于 float32 增加的稳定性。

paras.en: Smaller parameter initialization for stability. Appropriate initialization is critical to successful training in deep learning and we especially observe this to be true for Switch Transformer. We initialize our weight matrices by drawing elements from a truncated normal distribution with mean μ=0𝜇0\mu=0 and standard deviation σ=s/n𝜎𝑠𝑛\sigma=\sqrt{s/n} where s𝑠s is a scale hyper-parameter and n𝑛n is the number of input units in the weight tensor (e.g. fan-in).666Values greater than two standard deviations from the mean are resampled.
paras.zh: 更小的参数初始化以实现稳定性。适当的初始化对于深度学习中的成功训练至关重要，我们特别观察到这对于 Switch Transformer 也是如此。我们通过从截断正态分布中抽取元素来初始化权重矩阵，该分布的均值 μ=0，标准差 σ=√(s/n)，其中 s 是尺度超参数，n 是权重张量中的输入单元数（即扇入）。666 距离均值超过两个标准差的值会被重新采样。

paras.en: As an additional remedy to the instability, we recommend reducing the default Transformer initialization scale s=1.0𝑠1.0s=1.0 by a factor of 10. This both improves quality and reduces the likelihood of destabilized training in our experiments. Table 3 measures the improvement of the model quality and reduction of the variance early in training.
paras.zh: 作为不稳定性的额外补救措施，我们建议将默认 Transformer 初始化尺度 s=1.0 降低 10 倍。这既提高了质量，又降低了实验中训练不稳定的可能性。表 3 测量了模型质量的改进以及训练早期方差的减少。

paras.en: We find that the average model quality, as measured by the Neg. Log Perp., is dramatically improved and there is a far reduced variance across runs. Further, this same initialization scheme is broadly effective for models spanning several orders of magnitude. We use the same approach to stably train models as small as our 223M parameter baseline to enormous models in excess of one trillion parameters.
paras.zh: 我们发现，以负对数困惑度衡量的平均模型质量显著提高，并且不同运行之间的方差大大降低。此外，相同的初始化方案对于跨越几个数量级的模型广泛有效。我们使用相同的方法来稳定地训练从 2.23 亿参数基线到超过一万亿参数的巨大模型。

paras.en: Regularizing large sparse models. Our paper considers the common NLP approach of pre-training on a large corpus followed by fine-tuning on smaller downstream tasks such as summarization or question answering. One issue that naturally arises is overfitting since many fine-tuning tasks have very few examples. During fine-tuning of standard Transformers, Raffel et al. (2019) use dropout (Srivastava et al., 2014) at each layer to prevent overfitting. Our Switch Transformers have significantly more parameters than the FLOP matched dense baseline, which can lead to more severe overfitting on these smaller downstream tasks.
paras.zh: 正则化大型稀疏模型。本文考虑了常见的 NLP 方法：在大语料库上进行预训练，然后在较小的下游任务（如摘要或问答）上进行微调。自然出现的一个问题是过拟合，因为许多微调任务的样本非常少。在标准 Transformer 的微调过程中，Raffel 等人（2019）在每一层使用 dropout（Srivastava 等人，2014）来防止过拟合。我们的 Switch Transformer 的参数数量明显多于 FLOP 匹配的密集基线，这可能导致在这些较小的下游任务上出现更严重的过拟合。

paras.en: We thus propose a simple way to alleviate this issue during fine-tuning: increase the dropout inside the experts, which we name as expert dropout. During fine-tuning we simply increase the dropout rate by a significant amount only at the interim feed-forward computation at each expert layer. Table 4 has the results for our expert dropout protocol. We observe that simply increasing the dropout across all layers leads to worse performance. However, setting a smaller dropout rate (0.1) at non-expert layers and a much larger dropout rate (0.4) at expert layers leads to performance improvements on four smaller downstream tasks.
paras.zh: 因此，我们提出了一种简单的方法来缓解微调期间的这个问题：增加专家内部的 dropout，我们称之为专家 dropout。在微调期间，我们仅在每个专家层的中间前馈计算处显著增加 dropout 率。表 4 显示了我们的专家 dropout 协议的结果。我们观察到，简单地增加所有层的 dropout 会导致性能下降。然而，在非专家层设置较小的 dropout 率（0.1），在专家层设置较大的 dropout 率（0.4），可以在四个较小的下游任务上带来性能提升。

paras.en: We present a study of the scaling properties of the Switch Transformer architecture during pre-training. Per Kaplan et al. (2020), we consider a regime where the model is not bottlenecked by either the computational budget or amount of data. To avoid the data bottleneck, we use the large C4 corpus with over 180B target tokens (Raffel et al., 2019) and we train until diminishing returns are observed.
paras.zh: 我们研究了 Switch Transformer 架构在预训练期间的缩放属性。遵循 Kaplan 等人 (2020) 的方法，我们考虑模型不受计算预算或数据量瓶颈影响的场景。为避免数据瓶颈，我们使用包含超过 180B 目标词元的大型 C4 语料库 (Raffel 等人, 2019)，并训练至观察到收益递减为止。

paras.en: The number of experts is the most efficient dimension for scaling our model. Increasing the experts keeps the computational cost approximately fixed since the model only selects one expert per token, regardless of the number of experts to choose from. The router must compute a probability distribution over more experts, however, this is a lightweight computation of cost O​(dm​o​d​e​l×num experts)𝑂subscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙num expertsO(d_{model}\times\text{num experts}) where dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model} is the embedding dimension of tokens passed between the layers. In this section, we consider the scaling properties on a step-basis and a time-basis with a fixed computational budget.
paras.zh: 专家数量是缩放模型最高效的维度。增加专家数量大致保持计算成本不变，因为无论可供选择的专家数量有多少，模型每个词元只选择一个专家。路由器必须计算更多专家上的概率分布，但这是一个轻量级计算，其成本为 O(d_model × 专家数量)，其中 d_model 是层间传递的词元的嵌入维度。在本节中，我们考虑在固定计算预算下，基于步骤和基于时间的缩放属性。

paras.en: For a fixed training duration and computational budget, should one train a dense or a sparse model?
paras.zh: 在固定的训练时长和算力预算下，应该训练密集模型还是稀疏模型？

full.sec: 3.3 Scaling Versus a Larger Dense Model
full.secZh: 3.3 Scaling（规模扩张）与更大的稠密模型

paras.en: The above analysis shows that a computationally-matched dense model is outpaced by its Switch counterpart. Figure 6 considers a different scenario: what if we instead had allocated our resources to a larger dense model? We do so now, measuring Switch-Base against the next strong baseline, T5-Large. But despite T5-Large applying 3.5x more FLOPs per token, Switch-Base is still more sample efficient and yields a 2.5x speedup. Furthermore, more gains can be had simply by designing a new, larger sparse version, Switch-Large, which is FLOP-matched to T5-Large. We do this and demonstrate superior scaling and fine-tuning in the following section.
paras.zh: 上述分析表明，在算力匹配的情况下，稠密模型的表现不及对应的Switch模型。图6考虑了另一种场景：如果我们把资源分配给一个更大的稠密模型会怎样？我们现在进行这样的比较，将Switch-Base与下一个强基线模型T5-Large进行对比。尽管T5-Large每个词元消耗的FLOPs是Switch-Base的3.5倍，但Switch-Base仍然具有更高的样本效率，并实现了2.5倍的加速。此外，只需设计一个新的、更大的稀疏版本Switch-Large（其FLOPs与T5-Large匹配），就能获得更多收益。我们这样做了，并在下一节中展示了其卓越的Scaling（规模扩张）和微调性能。

paras.en: Section 3 demonstrated the superior scaling properties while pre-training, but we now validate that these gains translate to improved language learning abilities on downstream tasks. We begin by fine-tuning on a diverse set of NLP tasks. Next we study reducing the memory footprint of our sparse models by over 90% by distilling into small—and easily deployed—dense baselines. Finally, we conclude this section measuring the improvements in a multi-task, multilingual setting, where we show that Switch Transformers are strong multi-task learners, improving over the multilingual T5-base model across all 101 languages.
paras.zh: 第3节展示了预训练时优越的Scaling（规模扩张）特性，但我们现需验证这些增益能转化为下游任务中语言学习能力的提升。我们首先在一组多样化的NLP任务上进行微调。接着，我们研究通过蒸馏将稀疏模型的记忆占用降低90%以上，得到易于部署的小型密集基线模型。最后，我们通过测量多任务、多语言环境下的改进来总结本节，其中我们展示了Switch Transformers是强大的多任务学习者，在所有101种语言上均优于多语言T5-base模型。

paras.en: Baseline and Switch models used for fine-tuning. Our baselines are the highly-tuned 223M parameter T5-Base model and the 739M parameter T5-Large model (Raffel et al., 2019). For both versions, we design a FLOP-matched Switch Transformer, with many more parameters, which is summarized in Table 9.777FLOPS are calculated for the forward pass as done in Kaplan et al. (2020). Our baselines differ slightly from those in Raffel et al. (2019) because we pre-train on an improved C4 corpus which removes intra-example text duplication and thus increases the efficacy as a pre-training task Lee et al. (2021). In our protocol we pre-train with 220superscript2202^{20} (1,048,576) tokens per batch for 550k steps amounting to 576B total tokens. We then fine-tune across a diverse set of tasks using a dropout rate of 0.1 for all layers except the Switch layers, which use a dropout rate of 0.4 (see Table 4). We fine-tune using a batch-size of 1M for 16k steps and for each task, we evaluate model quality every 200-steps and report the peak performance as computed on the validation set.
paras.zh: 用于微调的基线模型和Switch模型。我们的基线是经过高度调优的223M参数T5-Base模型和739M参数T5-Large模型（Raffel等人，2019）。对于这两个版本，我们设计了一个FLOP匹配的Switch Transformer，其参数更多，总结在表9中。777FLOPs按Kaplan等人（2020）的方法计算前向传播。我们的基线与Raffel等人（2019）略有不同，因为我们在改进的C4语料库上进行预训练，该语料库去除了样本内的文本重复，从而提高了预训练任务的有效性（Lee等人，2021）。在我们的方案中，我们以每批220superscript220^{2}20（1,048,576）个token进行预训练，共550k步，总计576B个token。然后，我们在多样化的任务上进行微调，所有层使用0.1的dropout率，但Switch层使用0.4的dropout率（见表4）。我们使用1M的批量大小进行16k步的微调，对于每个任务，每200步评估一次模型质量，并报告在验证集上计算的峰值性能。

paras.en: Fine-tuning tasks and data sets. We select tasks probing language capabilities including question answering, summarization and knowledge about the world. The language benchmarks GLUE (Wang et al., 2018) and SuperGLUE (Wang et al., 2019) are handled as composite mixtures with all the tasks blended in proportion to the amount of tokens present in each. These benchmarks consist of tasks requiring sentiment analysis (SST-2), word sense disambiguation (WIC), sentence similarty (MRPC, STS-B, QQP), natural language inference (MNLI, QNLI, RTE, CB), question answering (MultiRC, RECORD, BoolQ), coreference resolution (WNLI, WSC) and sentence completion (COPA) and sentence acceptability (CoLA). The CNNDM (Hermann et al., 2015) and BBC XSum (Narayan et al., 2018) data sets are used to measure the ability to summarize articles. Question answering is probed with the SQuAD data set (Rajpurkar et al., 2016) and the ARC Reasoning Challenge (Clark et al., 2018). And as in Roberts et al. (2020), we evaluate the knowledge of our models by fine-tuning on three closed-book question answering data sets: Natural Questions (Kwiatkowski et al., 2019), Web Questions (Berant et al., 2013) and Trivia QA (Joshi et al., 2017). Closed-book refers to questions posed with no supplemental reference or context material. To gauge the model’s common sense reasoning we evaluate it on the Winogrande Schema Challenge (Sakaguchi et al., 2020). And finally, we test our model’s natural language inference capabilities on the Adversarial NLI Benchmark (Nie et al., 2019).
paras.zh: 微调任务和数据集。我们选择探索语言能力的任务，包括问答、摘要和世界知识。语言基准GLUE（Wang等人，2018）和SuperGLUE（Wang等人，2019）作为复合混合任务处理，所有任务按每个任务中token数量的比例混合。这些基准包括需要情感分析（SST-2）、词义消歧（WIC）、句子相似性（MRPC、STS-B、QQP）、自然语言推理（MNLI、QNLI、RTE、CB）、问答（MultiRC、RECORD、BoolQ）、共指消解（WNLI、WSC）、句子补全（COPA）和句子可接受性（CoLA）的任务。CNNDM（Hermann等人，2015）和BBC XSum（Narayan等人，2018）数据集用于衡量摘要文章的能力。问答通过SQuAD数据集（Rajpurkar等人，2016）和ARC推理挑战（Clark等人，2018）进行探索。与Roberts等人（2020）一样，我们通过在三个闭卷问答数据集上进行微调来评估模型的知识：Natural Questions（Kwiatkowski等人，2019）、Web Questions（Berant等人，2013）和Trivia QA（Joshi等人，2017）。闭卷指的是没有补充参考或上下文材料的问题。为了衡量模型的常识推理，我们在Winogrande模式挑战（Sakaguchi等人，2020）上进行评估。最后，我们在对抗性NLI基准（Nie等人，2019）上测试模型的自然语言推理能力。

paras.en: Fine-tuning metrics. The following evaluation metrics are used throughout the paper: We report the average scores across all subtasks for GLUE and SuperGLUE. The Rouge-2 metric is used both the CNNDM and XSum. In SQuAD and the closed book tasks (Web, Natural, and Trivia Questions) we report the percentage of answers exactly matching the target (refer to Roberts et al. (2020) for further details and deficiency of this measure). Finally, in ARC Easy, ARC Challenge, ANLI, and Winogrande we report the accuracy of the generated responses.
paras.zh: 微调指标。本文使用以下评估指标：我们报告GLUE和SuperGLUE所有子任务的平均得分。Rouge-2指标用于CNNDM和XSum。在SQuAD和闭卷任务（Web、Natural和Trivia Questions）中，我们报告答案与目标完全匹配的百分比（更多细节和该指标的缺陷请参考Roberts等人（2020））。最后，在ARC Easy、ARC Challenge、ANLI和Winogrande中，我们报告生成响应的准确率。

paras.en: Fine-tuning results. We observe significant downstream improvements across many natural language tasks. Notable improvements come from SuperGLUE, where we find FLOP-matched Switch variants improve by 4.4 and 2 percentage points over the T5-Base and T5-Large baselines, respectively as well as large improvements in Winogrande, closed book Trivia QA, and XSum.888Our T5 and Switch models were pre-trained with 220superscript2202^{20} tokens per batch for 550k steps on a revised C4 data set for fair comparisons. In our fine-tuning study, the only tasks where we do not observe gains are on the AI2 Reasoning Challenge (ARC) data sets where the T5-Base outperforms Switch-Base on the challenge data set and T5-Large outperforms Switch-Large on the easy data set. Taken as a whole, we observe significant improvements spanning both reasoning and knowledge-heavy tasks. This validates our architecture, not just as one that pre-trains well, but can translate quality improvements to downstream tasks via fine-tuning.
paras.zh: 微调结果。我们观察到许多自然语言任务在下游任务上有显著改进。显著的改进来自SuperGLUE，我们发现FLOP匹配的Switch变体分别比T5-Base和T5-Large基线提高了4.4和2个百分点，并且在Winogrande、闭卷Trivia QA和XSum上也有大幅改进。888我们的T5和Switch模型在修订后的C4数据集上以每批220superscript220^{2}20个token预训练了550k步，以实现公平比较。在我们的微调研究中，唯一没有观察到增益的任务是AI2推理挑战（ARC）数据集，其中T5-Base在挑战数据集上优于Switch-Base，T5-Large在简单数据集上优于Switch-Large。总体而言，我们观察到在推理和知识密集型任务上都有显著改进。这验证了我们的架构不仅预训练效果好，而且能够通过微调将质量改进转化为下游任务。

paras.en: Deploying massive neural networks with billions, or trillions, of parameters is inconvenient. To alleviate this, we study distilling (Hinton et al., 2015) large sparse models into small dense models. Future work could additionally study distilling large models into smaller sparse models.
paras.zh: 部署具有数十亿或数万亿参数的大规模神经网络并不方便。为了缓解这一问题，我们研究了将大型稀疏模型蒸馏（Hinton 等人，2015）为小型密集模型。未来的工作还可以进一步研究将大型模型蒸馏为更小的稀疏模型。

paras.en: Distillation techniques. In Table 6 we study a variety of distillation techniques. These techniques are built off of Sanh et al. (2019), who study distillation methods for BERT models. We find that initializing the dense model with the non-expert weights yields a modest improvement. This is possible since all models are FLOP matched, so non-expert layers will have the same dimensions. Since expert layers are usually only added at every or every other FFN layer in a Transformer, this allows for many of the weights to be initialized with trained parameters. Furthermore, we observe a distillation improvement using a mixture of 0.25 for the teacher probabilities and 0.75 for the ground truth label. By combining both techniques we preserve ≈\approx 30% of the quality gains from the larger sparse models with only ≈1/20t​habsent1superscript20𝑡ℎ\approx 1/20^{th} of the parameters. The quality gain refers to the percent of the quality difference between Switch-Base (Teacher) and T5-Base (Student). Therefore, a quality gain of 100% implies the Student equals the performance of the Teacher.
paras.zh: 蒸馏技术。在表6中，我们研究了多种蒸馏技术。这些技术基于 Sanh 等人（2019）对 BERT 模型蒸馏方法的研究。我们发现，用非专家权重初始化密集模型能带来适度的改进。这是可行的，因为所有模型的 FLOP 都匹配，所以非专家层具有相同的维度。由于在 Transformer 中，专家层通常只在每隔一个或每个 FFN 层添加，这使得许多权重可以用训练好的参数初始化。此外，我们观察到，使用教师概率 0.25 和真实标签 0.75 的混合比例进行蒸馏能带来改进。通过结合这两种技术，我们仅用约 1/20 的参数就保留了来自较大稀疏模型约 30% 的质量提升。质量提升指的是 Switch-Base（教师）和 T5-Base（学生）之间质量差异的百分比。因此，100% 的质量提升意味着学生达到了教师的性能。

paras.en: Achievable compression rates. Using our best distillation technique described in Table 6, we distill a wide variety of sparse models into dense models. We distill Switch-Base versions, sweeping over an increasing number of experts, which corresponds to varying between 1.1B to 14.7B parameters. Through distillation, we can preserve 37% of the quality gain of the 1.1B parameter model while compressing 82%. At the extreme, where we compress the model 99%, we are still able to maintain 28% of the teacher’s model quality improvement.
paras.zh: 可实现的压缩率。使用我们在表6中描述的最佳蒸馏技术，我们将各种稀疏模型蒸馏为密集模型。我们对 Switch-Base 版本进行蒸馏，扫描专家数量的增加，这对应于参数从 1.1B 到 14.7B 的变化。通过蒸馏，我们可以在压缩 82% 的同时保留 1.1B 参数模型质量提升的 37%。在极端情况下，我们将模型压缩 99%，仍然能够保持教师模型质量改进的 28%。

paras.en: Distilling a fine-tuned model. We conclude this with a study of distilling a fine-tuned sparse model into a dense model. Table 8 shows results of distilling a 7.4B parameter Switch-Base model, fine-tuned on the SuperGLUE task, into the 223M T5-Base. Similar to our pre-training results, we find we are able to preserve 30% of the gains of the sparse model when distilling into a FLOP matched dense variant. One potential future avenue, not considered here, may examine the specific experts being used for fine-tuning tasks and extracting them to achieve better model compression.
paras.zh: 蒸馏微调后的模型。我们最后研究了将微调后的稀疏模型蒸馏为密集模型。表8显示了将 7.4B 参数的 Switch-Base 模型（在 SuperGLUE 任务上微调）蒸馏为 223M 的 T5-Base 的结果。与我们的预训练结果类似，我们发现当蒸馏到 FLOP 匹配的密集变体时，我们能够保留稀疏模型 30% 的增益。未来一个潜在的途径（此处未考虑）可能是检查用于微调任务的特定专家，并提取它们以实现更好的模型压缩。

paras.en: In our final set of downstream experiments, we measure the model quality and speed tradeoffs while pre-training on a mixture of 101 different languages. We build and benchmark off the recent work of mT5 (Xue et al., 2020), a multilingual extension to T5. We pre-train on the multilingual variant of the Common Crawl data set (mC4) spanning 101 languages introduced in mT5, but due to script variants within certain languages, the mixture contains 107 tasks.
paras.zh: 在最后一组下游实验中，我们在101种不同语言的混合数据上预训练，衡量模型质量与速度的权衡。我们基于并参考了mT5（Xue等人，2020）的最新工作，这是T5的多语言扩展。我们在mT5中引入的涵盖101种语言的多语言Common Crawl数据集（mC4）上进行预训练，但由于某些语言内的文字变体，该混合数据包含107个任务。

paras.en: In Figure 7 we plot the quality improvement in negative log perplexity for all languages of a FLOP-matched Switch model, mSwitch-Base to the T5 base variant, mT5-Base. After pre-training both versions for 1M steps, we find that on all 101 languages considered, Switch Transformer increases the final negative log perplexity over the baseline. In Figure 8, we present a different view and now histogram the per step speed-up of using Switch Transformer over the mT5-Base.999The speedup on a step basis is computed as the ratio of the number of steps for the baseline divided by the number of steps required by our model to reach that same quality. We find a mean speed-up over mT5-Base of 5x and that 91% of languages achieve at least a 4x speedup. This presents evidence that Switch Transformers are effective multi-task and multi-lingual learners.
paras.zh: 在图7中，我们绘制了FLOP匹配的Switch模型（mSwitch-Base）与T5基础变体（mT5-Base）在所有语言上的负对数困惑度质量提升。在两种版本均预训练100万步后，我们发现，在所有101种语言上，Switch Transformer相比基线提高了最终的负对数困惑度。在图8中，我们展示了不同的视角，并绘制了使用Switch Transformer相比mT5-Base的每步加速直方图。999每步加速比计算为基线达到相同质量所需的步数除以我们模型所需的步数。我们发现，相比mT5-Base，平均加速比为5倍，且91%的语言实现了至少4倍的加速。这证明了Switch Transformer是有效的多任务和多语言学习器。

full.sec: 5 Designing Models with Data, Model, and Expert-Parallelism
full.secZh: 5 结合数据、模型和专家并行设计模型

paras.en: Arbitrarily increasing the number of experts is subject to diminishing returns (Figure 4). Here we describe complementary scaling strategies. The common way to scale a Transformer is to increase dimensions in tandem, like dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model} or df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff}. This increases both the parameters and computation performed and is ultimately limited by the memory per accelerator. Once it exceeds the size of the accelerator’s memory, single program multiple data (SPMD) model-parallelism can be employed. This section studies the trade-offs of combining data, model, and expert-parallelism.
paras.zh: 任意增加专家数量会面临收益递减（图4）。这里我们描述互补的缩放策略。缩放Transformer的常见方式是同步增加维度，如d_model或d_ff。这会同时增加参数和计算量，并最终受限于每个加速器的内存。一旦超过加速器内存大小，可以采用单程序多数据（SPMD）模型并行。本节研究结合数据、模型和专家并行的权衡。

paras.en: Reviewing the Feed-Forward Network (FFN) Layer. We use the FFN layer as an example of how data, model and expert-parallelism works in Mesh TensorFlow (Shazeer et al., 2018) and review it briefly here. We assume B𝐵B tokens in the batch, each of dimension dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}. Both the input (x𝑥x) and output (y𝑦y) of the FFN are of size [B𝐵B, dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}] and the intermediate (hℎh) is of size [B𝐵B, df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff}] where df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff} is typically several times larger than dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}. In the FFN, the intermediate is h=x​Wi​nℎ𝑥subscript𝑊𝑖𝑛h=xW_{in} and then the output of the layer is y=R​e​L​U​(h)​Wo​u​t𝑦𝑅𝑒𝐿𝑈ℎsubscript𝑊𝑜𝑢𝑡y=ReLU(h)W_{out}. Thus Wi​nsubscript𝑊𝑖𝑛W_{in} and Wo​u​tsubscript𝑊𝑜𝑢𝑡W_{out} are applied independently to each token and have sizes [dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}, df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff}] and [df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff}, dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}].
paras.zh: 回顾前馈网络（FFN）层。我们以FFN层为例说明数据、模型和专家并行在Mesh TensorFlow（Shazeer et al., 2018）中的工作方式，并在此简要回顾。假设批次中有B个token，每个维度为d_model。FFN的输入（x）和输出（y）大小均为[B, d_model]，中间变量（h）大小为[B, d_ff]，其中d_ff通常比d_model大几倍。在FFN中，中间变量h = xW_in，然后层输出y = ReLU(h)W_out。因此W_in和W_out独立应用于每个token，大小分别为[d_model, d_ff]和[d_ff, d_model]。

paras.en: We describe two aspects of partitioning: how the weights and batches of data divide over cores, depicted in Figure 9. We denote all cores available as N𝑁N which Mesh Tensorflow may then remap into a logical multidimensional mesh of processors. Here we create a two-dimensional logical mesh, with one dimension representing the number of ways for data-parallel sharding (n𝑛n) and the other, the model-parallel sharding (m𝑚m). The total cores must equal the ways to shard across both data and model-parallelism, e.g. N=n×m𝑁𝑛𝑚N=n\times m. To shard the layer across cores, the tensors containing that batch of B𝐵B tokens are sharded across n𝑛n data-parallel cores, so each core contains B/n𝐵𝑛B/n tokens. Tensors and variables with df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff} are then sharded across m𝑚m model-parallel cores. For the variants with experts-layers, we consider E𝐸E experts, each of which can process up to C𝐶C tokens.
paras.zh: 我们描述分区的两个方面：权重和数据批次如何在核心上划分，如图9所示。我们将所有可用核心记为N，Mesh TensorFlow可将其重新映射为逻辑多维处理器网格。这里我们创建一个二维逻辑网格，一个维度表示数据并行分片数（n），另一个表示模型并行分片数（m）。总核心数必须等于跨数据并行和模型并行的分片方式数，即N = n × m。为了在核心间分片该层，包含该批次B个token的张量在n个数据并行核心上分片，因此每个核心包含B/n个token。然后，具有d_ff维度的张量和变量在m个模型并行核心上分片。对于包含专家层的变体，我们考虑E个专家，每个专家最多可处理C个token。

paras.en: When training data parallel models, which is the standard for distributed training, then all cores are allocated to the data-parallel dimension or n=N,m=1formulae-sequence𝑛𝑁𝑚1n=N,m=1. This has the advantage that no communication is needed until the entire forward and backward pass is finished and the gradients need to be then aggregated across all cores. This corresponds to the left-most column of Figure 9.
paras.zh: 在训练数据并行模型（这是分布式训练的标准做法）时，所有核心都分配给数据并行维度，即 n=N,m=1。其优点是在整个前向和反向传播完成之前无需通信，之后才需要跨所有核心聚合梯度。这对应于图9的最左列。

paras.en: We now consider a scenario where all cores are allocated exclusively to the model-parallel dimension and so n=1,m=Nformulae-sequence𝑛1𝑚𝑁n=1,m=N. Now all cores must keep the full B𝐵B tokens and each core will contain a unique slice of the weights. For each forward and backward pass, a communication cost is now incurred. Each core sends a tensor of [B𝐵B, dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}] to compute the second matrix multiplication R​e​L​U​(h)​Wo​u​t𝑅𝑒𝐿𝑈ℎsubscript𝑊𝑜𝑢𝑡ReLU(h)W_{out} because the df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff} dimension is partitioned and must be summed over. As a general rule, whenever a dimension that is partitioned across cores must be summed, then an all-reduce operation is added for both the forward and backward pass. This contrasts with pure data parallelism where an all-reduce only occurs at the end of the entire forward and backward pass.
paras.zh: 现在我们考虑所有核心都专门分配给模型并行维度的情况，因此 n=1, m=N。现在所有核心必须保留完整的 B 个 token，每个核心将包含权重的唯一切片。对于每个前向和反向传播，现在会产生通信成本。每个核心发送一个大小为 [B, d_model] 的张量来计算第二个矩阵乘法 ReLU(h)W_out，因为 d_ff 维度被分区并且必须求和。作为一般规则，每当跨核心分区的维度必须求和时，就会在前向和反向传播中添加一个全规约操作。这与纯数据并行形成对比，在纯数据并行中，全规约仅在整个前向和反向传播结束时发生。

paras.en: It is common to mix both model and data parallelism for large scale models, which was done in the largest T5 models (Raffel et al., 2019; Xue et al., 2020) and in GPT-3 (Brown et al., 2020). With a total of N=n×m𝑁𝑛𝑚N=n\times m cores, now each core will be responsible for B/n𝐵𝑛B/n tokens and df​f/msubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓𝑚d_{ff}/m of both the weights and intermediate activation. In the forward and backward pass each core communicates a tensor of size [B/n,dm​o​d​e​l]𝐵𝑛subscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙[B/n,d_{model}] in an all-reduce operation.
paras.zh: 对于大规模模型，通常混合使用模型并行和数据并行，这在最大的 T5 模型（Raffel 等人，2019；Xue 等人，2020）和 GPT-3（Brown 等人，2020）中均有采用。假设总共有 N=n×m 个核心，每个核心将负责 B/n 个词元以及 d_ff/m 的权重和中间激活。在前向和反向传播中，每个核心通过 all-reduce 操作通信一个大小为 [B/n, d_model] 的张量。

paras.en: Next we describe the partitioning strategy for expert and data parallelism. Switch Transformers will allocate all of their cores to the data partitioning dimension n𝑛n, which will also correspond to the number of experts in the model. For each token per core a router locally computes assignments to the experts. The output is a binary matrix of size [n𝑛n, B/n𝐵𝑛B/n, E𝐸E, C𝐶C] which is partitioned across the first dimension and determines expert assignment. This binary matrix is then used to do a gather via matrix multiplication with the input tensor of [n𝑛n, B/n𝐵𝑛B/n, dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}].
paras.zh: 接下来我们描述专家并行和数据并行的划分策略。Switch Transformer 会将其所有核心分配到数据划分维度 n 上，该维度也将对应模型中的专家数量。对于每个核心上的每个词元，路由器会在本地计算到专家的分配。输出是一个大小为 [n, B/n, E, C] 的二进制矩阵，该矩阵沿第一个维度划分，并决定专家分配。然后，通过矩阵乘法将该二进制矩阵与大小为 [n, B/n, d_model] 的输入张量进行收集操作。

paras.en: resulting in the final tensor of shape [n𝑛n, E𝐸E, C𝐶C, dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}], which is sharded across the first dimension. Because each core has its own expert, we do an all-to-all communication of size [E𝐸E, C𝐶C, dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}] to now shard the E𝐸E dimension instead of the n𝑛n-dimension. There are additional communication costs of bfloat16 tensors of size E×C×dm​o​d​e​l𝐸𝐶subscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙E\times C\times d_{model} in the forward pass to analogusly receive the tokens from each expert located on different cores. See Appendix F for a detailed analysis of the expert partitioning code.
paras.zh: 得到最终形状为 [n, E, C, d_model] 的张量，该张量沿第一个维度分片。由于每个核心拥有自己的专家，我们执行大小为 [E, C, d_model] 的全到全通信，以便现在沿 E 维度分片，而不是 n 维度。在前向传播中，还存在额外的通信开销，即大小为 E×C×d_model 的 bfloat16 张量，用于类似地从位于不同核心上的每个专家接收词元。有关专家划分代码的详细分析，请参见附录 F。

paras.en: In the design of our best model, we seek to balance the FLOPS per token and the parameter count. When we scale the number of experts, we increase the number of parameters, but do not change the FLOPs per token. In order to increase FLOPs, we must also increase the df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff} dimension (which also increases parameters, but at a slower rate). This presents a trade-off: as we increase df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff} we will run out of memory per core, which then necessitates increasing m𝑚m. But since we have a fixed number of cores N𝑁N, and N=n×m𝑁𝑛𝑚N=n\times m, we must decrease n𝑛n, which forces use of a smaller batch-size (in order to hold tokens per core constant).
paras.zh: 在我们最佳模型的设计中，我们力求平衡每个token的FLOPS和参数数量。当我们扩展专家数量时，参数数量增加，但每个token的FLOPs不变。为了增加FLOPs，我们还必须增加d_ff维度（这也会增加参数，但速度较慢）。这带来了一个权衡：随着d_ff的增加，每个核心的内存将耗尽，从而需要增加m。但由于我们拥有固定数量的核心N，且N = n × m，我们必须减少n，这迫使使用更小的批量大小（以保持每个核心的token数恒定）。

paras.en: When combining both model and expert-parallelism, we will have all-to-all communication costs from routing the tokens to the correct experts along with the internal all-reduce communications from the model parallelism. Balancing the FLOPS, communication costs and memory per core becomes quite complex when combining all three methods where the best mapping is empirically determined. See our further analysis in section 5.6 for how the number of experts effects the downstream performance as well.
paras.zh: 当同时结合模型并行和专家并行时，我们将面临将token路由到正确专家的全对全通信成本，以及模型并行带来的内部全规约通信。当结合所有三种方法时，平衡FLOPS、通信成本和每个核心的内存变得非常复杂，最佳映射通过经验确定。关于专家数量如何影响下游性能的进一步分析，请参见第5.6节。

paras.en: Combining expert, model and data parallelism, we design two large Switch Transformer models, one with 395 billion and 1.6 trillion parameters, respectively. We study how these models perform on both up-stream pre-training as language models and their downstream fine-tuning performance. The parameters, FLOPs per sequence and hyper-parameters of the two different models are listed below in Table 9. Standard hyper-parameters of the Transformer, including dm​o​d​e​lsubscript𝑑𝑚𝑜𝑑𝑒𝑙d_{model}, df​fsubscript𝑑𝑓𝑓d_{ff}, dk​vsubscript𝑑𝑘𝑣d_{kv}, number of heads and number of layers are described, as well as a less common feature, F​F​NG​E​G​L​U𝐹𝐹subscript𝑁𝐺𝐸𝐺𝐿𝑈FFN_{GEGLU}, which refers to a variation of the FFN layer where the expansion matrix is substituted with two sets of weights which are non-linearly combined (Shazeer, 2020).
paras.zh: 结合专家并行、模型并行和数据并行，我们设计了两个大型 Switch Transformer 模型，参数分别为 3950 亿和 1.6 万亿。我们研究了这些模型在上游预训练作为语言模型以及下游微调性能方面的表现。两个模型的参数、每序列 FLOPs 和超参数如下表 9 所示。描述了 Transformer 的标准超参数，包括 d_model、d_ff、d_kv、头数和层数，以及一个不太常见的特征 FFN_GEGLU，它指的是 FFN 层的一种变体，其中扩展矩阵被两组权重替代，这些权重通过非线性方式组合（Shazeer, 2020）。

paras.en: The Switch-C model is designed using only expert-parallelism, and no model-parallelism, as described earlier in Section 5.4. As a result, the hyper-parameters controlling the width, depth, number of heads, and so on, are all much smaller than the T5-XXL model. In contrast, the Switch-XXL is FLOP-matched to the T5-XXL model, which allows for larger dimensions of the hyper-parameters, but at the expense of additional communication costs induced by model-parallelism (see Section 5.5 for more details).
paras.zh: Switch-C 模型仅使用专家并行，而不使用模型并行，如前文第 5.4 节所述。因此，控制宽度、深度、头数等的超参数都远小于 T5-XXL 模型。相比之下，Switch-XXL 在 FLOPs 上与 T5-XXL 模型匹配，这使得超参数的维度可以更大，但代价是模型并行带来的额外通信开销（详见第 5.5 节）。

paras.en: Sample efficiency versus T5-XXL. In the final two columns of Table 9 we record the negative log perplexity on the C4 corpus after 250k and 500k steps, respectively. After 250k steps, we find both Switch Transformer variants to improve over the T5-XXL version’s negative log perplexity by over 0.061.101010This reported quality difference is a lower bound, and may actually be larger. The T5-XXL was pre-trained on an easier C4 data set which included duplicated, and thus easily copied, snippets within examples. To contextualize the significance of a gap of 0.061, we note that the T5-XXL model had to train for an additional 250k steps to increase 0.052. The gap continues to increase with additional training, with the Switch-XXL model out-performing the T5-XXL by 0.087 by 500k steps.
paras.zh: 与 T5-XXL 的样本效率比较。在表 9 的最后两列中，我们分别记录了在 C4 语料库上经过 250k 步和 500k 步后的负对数困惑度。经过 250k 步后，我们发现两个 Switch Transformer 变体相比 T5-XXL 版本的负对数困惑度提高了超过 0.061。¹⁰ 这个报告的质量差异是一个下界，实际可能更大。T5-XXL 是在一个更简单的 C4 数据集上预训练的，该数据集包含重复的、因此容易复制的片段。为了说明 0.061 差距的重要性，我们注意到 T5-XXL 模型需要额外训练 250k 步才能提高 0.052。随着训练的继续，差距进一步扩大，到 500k 步时，Switch-XXL 模型比 T5-XXL 高出 0.087。

paras.en: Training instability. However, as described in the introduction, large sparse models can be unstable, and as we increase the scale, we encounter some sporadic issues. We find that the larger Switch-C model, with 1.6T parameters and 2048 experts, exhibits no training instability at all. Instead, the Switch XXL version, with nearly 10x larger FLOPs per sequence, is sometimes unstable. As a result, though this is our better model on a step-basis, we do not pre-train for a full 1M steps, in-line with the final reported results of T5 (Raffel et al., 2019).
paras.zh: 训练不稳定性。然而，如引言所述，大型稀疏模型可能不稳定，随着规模扩大，我们遇到了一些零星问题。我们发现更大的 Switch-C 模型（1.6T 参数，2048 个专家）完全没有表现出训练不稳定性。相反，Switch-XXL 版本（每序列 FLOPs 大了近 10 倍）有时不稳定。因此，尽管这是我们在步数上更好的模型，但我们没有预训练完整的 1M 步，这与 T5 的最终报告结果一致（Raffel 等人，2019）。

paras.en: Reasoning fine-tuning performance. As a preliminary assessment of the model quality, we use a Switch-XXL model partially pre-trained on 503B tokens, or approximately half the text used by the T5-XXL model. Using this checkpoint, we conduct multi-task training for efficiency, where all tasks are learned jointly, rather than individually fine-tuned. We find that SQuAD accuracy on the validation set increases to 89.7 versus state-of-the-art of 91.3. Next, the average SuperGLUE test score is recorded at 87.5 versus the T5 version obtaining a score of 89.3 compared to the state-of-the-art of 90.0 (Wang et al., 2019). On ANLI (Nie et al., 2019), Switch XXL improves over the prior state-of-the-art to get a 65.7 accuracy versus the prior best of 49.4 (Yang et al., 2020). We note that while the Switch-XXL has state-of-the-art Neg. Log Perp. on the upstream pre-training task, its gains have not yet fully translated to SOTA downstream performance. We study this issue more in Appendix E.
paras.zh: 推理微调性能。作为模型质量的初步评估，我们使用了一个部分预训练了 503B 个 token（约为 T5-XXL 模型所用文本的一半）的 Switch-XXL 模型。利用这个检查点，我们进行多任务训练以提高效率，所有任务联合学习，而不是单独微调。我们发现验证集上的 SQuAD 准确率提高到 89.7，而之前的最优结果为 91.3。接下来，平均 SuperGLUE 测试得分为 87.5，而 T5 版本得分为 89.3，最优结果为 90.0（Wang 等人，2019）。在 ANLI 上（Nie 等人，2019），Switch-XXL 将之前的最优结果从 49.4（Yang 等人，2020）提高到 65.7。我们注意到，虽然 Switch-XXL 在上游预训练任务上具有最先进的负对数困惑度，但其增益尚未完全转化为最先进的下游性能。我们在附录 E 中进一步研究了这个问题。

paras.en: Knowledge-based fine-tuning performance. Finally, we also conduct an early examination of the model’s knowledge with three closed-book knowledge-based tasks: Natural Questions, WebQuestions and TriviaQA, without additional pre-training using Salient Span Masking (Guu et al., 2020). In all three cases, we observe improvements over the prior state-of-the-art T5-XXL model (without SSM). Natural Questions exact match increases to 34.4 versus the prior best of 32.8, Web Questions increases to 41.0 over 37.2, and TriviaQA increases to 47.5 versus 42.9.
paras.zh: 基于知识的微调性能。最后，我们还对模型的知识进行了早期检验，使用了三个闭卷知识型任务：Natural Questions、WebQuestions 和 TriviaQA，没有使用 Salient Span Masking（Guu 等人，2020）进行额外预训练。在所有三个任务中，我们观察到相比之前最先进的 T5-XXL 模型（无 SSM）有所改进。Natural Questions 的精确匹配从之前的 32.8 提高到 34.4，Web Questions 从 37.2 提高到 41.0，TriviaQA 从 42.9 提高到 47.5。

paras.en: Summing up, despite training on less than half the data of other models, we already find comparable, and sometimes state-of-the-art, model quality. Currently, the Switch Transformer translates substantial upstream gains better to knowledge-based tasks, than reasoning-tasks (see Appendix E). Extracting stronger fine-tuning performance from large expert models is an active research question, and the pre-training perplexity indicates future improvements should be possible.
paras.zh: 总结来说，尽管训练数据不到其他模型的一半，我们已经发现模型质量相当，有时甚至达到最先进水平。目前，Switch Transformer 将上游的显著增益更好地转化为知识型任务，而非推理型任务（见附录 E）。从大型专家模型中提取更强的微调性能是一个活跃的研究问题，预训练困惑度表明未来应该能够实现进一步的改进。

paras.en: The importance of scale in neural networks is widely recognized and several approaches have been proposed. Recent works have scaled models to billions of parameters through using model parallelism (e.g. splitting weights and tensors across multiple cores) (Shazeer et al., 2018; Rajbhandari et al., 2019; Raffel et al., 2019; Brown et al., 2020; Shoeybi et al., 2019). Alternatively, Harlap et al. (2018); Huang et al. (2019) propose using pipeline based model parallelism, where different layers are split across devices and micro-batches are pipelined to the different layers. Finally, Product Key networks (Lample et al., 2019) were proposed to scale up the capacity of neural networks by doing a lookup for learnable embeddings based on the incoming token representations to a given layer.
paras.zh: 神经网络中规模（scale）的重要性已被广泛认可，并提出了多种方法。近期工作通过使用模型并行（例如将权重和张量拆分到多个核心上）将模型扩展到数十亿参数（Shazeer et al., 2018; Rajbhandari et al., 2019; Raffel et al., 2019; Brown et al., 2020; Shoeybi et al., 2019）。另外，Harlap et al. (2018); Huang et al. (2019) 提出了基于流水线的模型并行，其中不同层被拆分到不同设备上，微批次被流水线化到不同层。最后，Product Key 网络（Lample et al., 2019）被提出，通过根据输入到给定层的词元表示查找可学习嵌入来扩展神经网络的容量。

paras.en: Our work studies a specific model in a class of methods that do conditional computation, where computation decisions are made dynamically based on the input. Cho and Bengio (2014) proposed adaptively selecting weights based on certain bit patterns occuring in the model hidden-states. Eigen et al. (2013) built stacked expert layers with dense matrix multiplications and ReLU activations and showed promising results on jittered MNIST and monotone speech. In computer vision Puigcerver et al. (2020) manually route tokens based on semantic classes during upstream pre-training and then select the relevant experts to be used according to the downstream task.
paras.zh: 我们的工作研究了一类条件计算方法中的特定模型，其中计算决策基于输入动态做出。Cho 和 Bengio (2014) 提出根据模型隐藏状态中出现的特定比特模式自适应地选择权重。Eigen et al. (2013) 构建了具有密集矩阵乘法和 ReLU 激活的堆叠专家层，并在抖动 MNIST 和单调语音上展示了有希望的结果。在计算机视觉中，Puigcerver et al. (2020) 在上游预训练期间根据语义类别手动路由词元，然后根据下游任务选择要使用的相关专家。

paras.en: Mixture of Experts (MoE), in the context of modern deep learning architectures, was proven effective in Shazeer et al. (2017). That work added an MoE layer which was stacked between LSTM (Hochreiter and Schmidhuber, 1997) layers, and tokens were separately routed to combinations of experts. This resulted in state-of-the-art results in language modeling and machine translation benchmarks. The MoE layer was reintroduced into the Transformer architecture by the Mesh Tensorflow library (Shazeer et al., 2018) where MoE layers were introduced as a substitute of the FFN layers, however, there were no accompanying NLP results. More recently, through advances in machine learning infrastructure, GShard (Lepikhin et al., 2020), which extended the XLA compiler, used the MoE Transformer to dramatically improve machine translation across 100 languages. Finally Fan et al. (2021) chooses a different deterministic MoE strategy to split the model parameters into non-overlapping groups of languages.
paras.zh: 在现代深度学习架构的背景下，混合专家模型（MoE）在 Shazeer et al. (2017) 中被证明是有效的。该工作添加了一个 MoE 层，堆叠在 LSTM（Hochreiter 和 Schmidhuber, 1997）层之间，并且词元被分别路由到专家组合。这在语言建模和机器翻译基准上取得了最先进的结果。Mesh Tensorflow 库（Shazeer et al., 2018）将 MoE 层重新引入 Transformer 架构，作为 FFN 层的替代，但没有附带 NLP 结果。最近，通过机器学习基础设施的进步，扩展了 XLA 编译器的 GShard（Lepikhin et al., 2020）使用 MoE Transformer 显著改进了跨 100 种语言的机器翻译。最后，Fan et al. (2021) 选择了一种不同的确定性 MoE 策略，将模型参数拆分为非重叠的语言组。

paras.en: Sparsity along the sequence length dimension (L𝐿L) in the Transformer attention patterns has been a successful technique to reduce the attention complexity from O​(L2)𝑂superscript𝐿2O(L^{2}) (Child et al., 2019; Correia et al., 2019; Sukhbaatar et al., 2019; Kitaev et al., 2020; Zaheer et al., 2020; Beltagy et al., 2020). This has enabled learning longer sequences than previously possible. This version of the Switch Transformer does not employ attention sparsity, but these techniques are complimentary, and, as future work, these could be combined to potentially improve learning on tasks requiring long contexts.
paras.zh: Transformer 注意力模式中沿序列长度维度（L）的稀疏性是一种成功的技术，可将注意力复杂度从 O(L²) 降低（Child et al., 2019; Correia et al., 2019; Sukhbaatar et al., 2019; Kitaev et al., 2020; Zaheer et al., 2020; Beltagy et al., 2020）。这使得学习比以往更长的序列成为可能。本版本的 Switch Transformer 没有采用注意力稀疏性，但这些技术是互补的，作为未来工作，可以结合它们以潜在地改进需要长上下文的任务的学习。

paras.en: We pose and discuss questions about the Switch Transformer, and sparse expert models generally, where sparsity refers to weights, not on attention patterns.
paras.zh: 我们提出并讨论关于 Switch Transformer 以及一般稀疏专家模型的问题，其中稀疏性指的是权重，而非注意力模式。

paras.en: Isn’t Switch Transformer better due to sheer parameter count? Yes, and by design! Parameters, independent of the total FLOPs used, are a useful axis to scale neural language models. Large models have been exhaustively shown to perform better (Kaplan et al., 2020). But in this case, our model is more sample efficient and faster while using the same computational resources.
paras.zh: Switch Transformer 是否仅仅因为参数数量更多而表现更好？是的，这正是设计初衷！参数（独立于总 FLOPs）是扩展神经语言模型的一个有用维度。大量研究已充分证明大型模型表现更优（Kaplan 等人，2020）。但在本工作中，我们的模型在使用相同计算资源的情况下，样本效率更高且速度更快。

paras.en: I don’t have access to a supercomputer—is this still useful for me? Though this work has focused on extremely large models, we also find that models with as few as two experts improves performance while easily fitting within memory constraints of commonly available GPUs or TPUs (details in Appendix D). We therefore believe our techniques are useful in small-scale settings.
paras.zh: 我没有超级计算机——这对我有用吗？尽管本工作聚焦于超大规模模型，但我们发现，即使只有两个专家的模型也能提升性能，同时轻松适配常见 GPU 或 TPU 的内存限制（详见附录 D）。因此，我们相信我们的技术在小规模场景下同样有用。

paras.en: Do sparse models outperform dense models on the speed-accuracy Pareto curve? Yes. Across a wide variety of different models sizes, sparse models outperform dense models per step and on wall clock time. Our controlled experiments show for a fixed amount of computation and time, sparse models outperform dense models.
paras.zh: 稀疏模型在速度-准确率帕累托曲线上是否优于稠密模型？是的。在各种不同规模的模型中，稀疏模型在每一步和实际时间上都优于稠密模型。我们的控制实验表明，在固定计算量和时间下，稀疏模型优于稠密模型。

paras.en: I can’t deploy a trillion parameter model—can we shrink these models? We cannot fully preserve the model quality, but compression rates of 10 to 100x are achievable by distilling our sparse models into dense models while achieving ≈\approx30% of the quality gain of the expert model.
paras.zh: 我无法部署万亿参数模型——能否缩小这些模型？我们无法完全保留模型质量，但通过将稀疏模型蒸馏为稠密模型，可实现 10 到 100 倍的压缩率，同时保留约 30% 的专家模型质量提升。

paras.en: Why use Switch Transformer instead of a model-parallel dense model? On a time basis, Switch Transformers can be far more efficient than dense-models with sharded parameters (Figure 6). Also, we point out that this decision is not mutually exclusive—we can, and do, use model-parallelism in Switch Transformers, increasing the FLOPs per token, but incurring the slowdown of conventional model-parallelism.
paras.zh: 为什么使用 Switch Transformer 而不是模型并行的稠密模型？在时间上，Switch Transformer 可以比参数分片的稠密模型高效得多（图 6）。此外，我们指出这一决策并非互斥——我们可以在 Switch Transformer 中使用模型并行，增加每个 token 的 FLOPs，但会带来传统模型并行的减速。

paras.en: Why aren’t sparse models widely used already? The motivation to try sparse models has been stymied by the massive success of scaling dense models (the success of which is partially driven by co-adaptation with deep learning hardware as argued in Hooker (2020)). Further, sparse models have been subject to multiple issues including (1) model complexity, (2) training difficulties, and (3) communication costs. Switch Transformer makes strides to alleviate these issues.
paras.zh: 为什么稀疏模型尚未广泛使用？尝试稀疏模型的动力因稠密模型扩展的巨大成功而受阻（其成功部分归因于与深度学习硬件的协同适应，如 Hooker (2020) 所述）。此外，稀疏模型面临多个问题，包括（1）模型复杂性、（2）训练困难以及（3）通信成本。Switch Transformer 在缓解这些问题方面取得了进展。

paras.en: This paper lays out a simplified architecture, improved training procedures, and a study of how sparse models scale. However, there remain many open future directions which we briefly describe here:
paras.zh: 本文提出了一种简化的架构、改进的训练过程，并研究了稀疏模型的缩放规律。然而，仍有许多开放性的未来方向，我们在此简要描述：

paras.en: A significant challenge is further improving training stability for the largest models. While our stability techniques were effective for our Switch-Base, Switch-Large and Switch-C models (no observed instability), they were not sufficient for Switch-XXL. We have taken early steps towards stabilizing these models, which we think may be generally useful for large models, including using regularizers for improving stability and adapted forms of gradient clipping, but this remains unsolved.
paras.zh: 一个重大挑战是进一步改善最大模型的训练稳定性。虽然我们的稳定性技术对 Switch-Base、Switch-Large 和 Switch-C 模型有效（未观察到不稳定性），但对 Switch-XXL 却不够。我们已采取初步措施来稳定这些模型，这些措施可能对大型模型普遍有用，包括使用正则化器提高稳定性和改进形式的梯度裁剪，但这一问题仍未解决。

paras.en: Generally we find that improved pre-training quality leads to better downstream results (Appendix E), though we sometimes encounter striking anomalies. For instance, despite similar perplexities modeling the C4 data set, the 1.6T parameter Switch-C achieves only an 87.7 exact match score in SQuAD, which compares unfavorably to 89.6 for the smaller Switch-XXL model. One notable difference is that the Switch-XXL model applies ≈\approx10x the FLOPS per token than the Switch-C model, even though it has ≈\approx4x less unique parameters (395B vs 1.6T). This suggests a poorly understood dependence between fine-tuning quality, FLOPS per token and number of parameters.
paras.zh: 总体而言，我们发现改进预训练质量会带来更好的下游结果（附录 E），尽管有时会遇到显著的异常。例如，尽管在 C4 数据集上建模困惑度相似，但 1.6T 参数的 Switch-C 在 SQuAD 中仅达到 87.7 的精确匹配分数，而较小的 Switch-XXL 模型为 89.6。一个显著区别是，Switch-XXL 模型每个 token 应用的 FLOPS 约为 Switch-C 的 10 倍，尽管其独特参数少了约 4 倍（395B 对 1.6T）。这表明微调质量、每个 token 的 FLOPS 和参数数量之间存在尚未充分理解的依赖关系。

paras.en: Perform a comprehensive study of scaling relationships to guide the design of architectures blending data, model and expert-parallelism. Ideally, given the specs of a hardware configuration (computation, memory, communication) one could more rapidly design an optimal model. And, vice versa, this may also help in the design of future hardware.
paras.zh: 对缩放关系进行全面研究，以指导融合数据、模型和专家并行性的架构设计。理想情况下，给定硬件配置的规格（计算、内存、通信），可以更快地设计出最优模型。反之，这也可能有助于未来硬件的设计。

paras.en: Our work falls within the family of adaptive computation algorithms. Our approach always used identical, homogeneous experts, but future designs (facilitated by more flexible infrastructure) could support heterogeneous experts. This would enable more flexible adaptation by routing to larger experts when more computation is desired—perhaps for harder examples.
paras.zh: 我们的工作属于自适应计算算法家族。我们的方法始终使用相同、同质的专家，但未来的设计（借助更灵活的基础设施）可以支持异质专家。这将通过将路由到更大的专家（可能针对更难的示例）来实现更灵活的自适应。

paras.en: Investigating expert layers outside the FFN layer of the Transformer. We find preliminary evidence that this similarly can improve model quality. In Appendix A, we report quality improvement adding these inside Self-Attention layers, where our layer replaces the weight matrices which produce Q, K, V. However, due to training instabilities with the bfloat16 format, we instead leave this as an area for future work.
paras.zh: 研究 Transformer 的 FFN 层之外的专家层。我们初步发现这同样可以改善模型质量。在附录 A 中，我们报告了在自注意力层内部添加这些层带来的质量提升，其中我们的层替换了产生 Q、K、V 的权重矩阵。然而，由于 bfloat16 格式下的训练不稳定性，我们将其留作未来工作。

paras.en: Examining Switch Transformer in new and across different modalities. We have thus far only considered language, but we believe that model sparsity can similarly provide advantages in new modalities, as well as multi-modal networks.
paras.zh: 探索 Switch Transformer 在新模态和跨不同模态中的应用。迄今为止我们只考虑了语言，但我们相信模型稀疏性同样可以在新模态以及多模态网络中带来优势。

paras.en: This list could easily be extended, but we hope this gives a flavor for the types of challenges that we are thinking about and what we suspect are promising future directions.
paras.zh: 这个列表可以很容易地扩展，但我们希望这能让我们了解我们正在思考的挑战类型以及我们认为有前景的未来方向。

paras.en: Switch Transformers are scalable and effective natural language learners. We simplify Mixture of Experts to produce an architecture that is easy to understand, stable to train and vastly more sample efficient than equivalently-sized dense models. We find that these models excel across a diverse set of natural language tasks and in different training regimes, including pre-training, fine-tuning and multi-task training. These advances make it possible to train models with hundreds of billion to trillion parameters and which achieve substantial speedups relative to dense T5 baselines. We hope our work motivates sparse models as an effective architecture and that this encourages researchers and practitioners to consider these flexible models in natural language tasks, and beyond.
paras.zh: Switch Transformers 是可扩展且有效的自然语言学习器。我们简化了混合专家模型，产生了一种易于理解、训练稳定且样本效率远高于同等规模稠密模型的架构。我们发现这些模型在多种自然语言任务和不同的训练模式（包括预训练、微调和多任务训练）中表现出色。这些进展使得训练具有数千亿到数万亿参数的模型成为可能，并且相对于稠密的 T5 基线实现了显著的加速。我们希望我们的工作能够激励稀疏模型作为一种有效的架构，并鼓励研究人员和从业者在自然语言任务及其他领域考虑这些灵活的模型。
